post.summary:
DeleteUser deletes a user (and associated OTP/verification data) by email.
Requires super-admin auth.

AdminLogin validates the admin secret and establishes an admin session
(Set-Cookie for browser callers). Public entry point — the ONLY admin RPC
that does not require an existing super-admin session.
AdminLogout clears the admin session cookie. Requires super-admin auth.

get.summary:
AdminMeta returns admin-only configuration metadata (configured roles,
default roles, protected roles). Requires super-admin auth.
AdminSession refreshes the admin session cookie. Requires super-admin auth.

post.summary:
UpdateUser updates a user's profile, roles, MFA, or verification state and
returns the updated user. Requires super-admin auth.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
UpdateUserRequest mirrors model.UpdateUserRequest. Nullable GraphQL inputs use
proto3 `optional` so an unset field is distinguishable from an empty value
(e.g. clearing a name vs leaving it untouched, or email_verified=false).

post.summary: User returns a single user by id or email. Requires super-admin auth.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: UserRequest fetches a single user by id or email; at least one must be set.

post.summary: Users returns a paginated list of all users. Requires super-admin auth.

VerificationRequests returns a paginated list of pending verification
requests. Requires super-admin auth.

CheckPermissions evaluates one or more fine-grained permission checks
("does the subject have <relation> on <object>?") in a single call and
returns one result per check, in order. The subject defaults to the
authenticated caller; an explicit `user` is honored only for super-admins
or when it equals the caller's own subject. Fail-closed.

DeactivateAccount soft-deletes the caller's account and revokes all
refresh tokens as a side effect. OAuth has no concept of account
deactivation; this is the typed equivalent of SCIM PATCH active=false.

ListPermissions enumerates what the subject can access. With both
`relation` and `object_type` set it answers "which <object_type>s can I
<relation>?"; either or both filters may be omitted to enumerate every
matching (type, relation) pair of the active model. Results are capped;
`truncated` reports when more permissions exist. Subject resolution
follows the same rules as CheckPermissions.

Logout ends the caller's current session. POST, not GET: logout mutates
server state (clears the session) and is audit-logged, so it must not be
a "safe" method per RFC 9110 §9.2.1 — GET would also expose it to CSRF.

MagicLinkLogin dispatches a passwordless email link. The clicked link
hits the existing /verify_email browser handler.

get.summary: Meta returns server feature flags. No auth required.

post.summary: Revoke invalidates a refresh token. Typed mirror of RFC 7009.

Session returns the AuthResponse bound to the caller's cookie/bearer.
NOT exposed as an MCP tool — SessionResponse carries access_token,
refresh_token, id_token, authenticator_secret, and recovery codes,
none of which should land in an LLM transcript. (Security audit C1.)

Signup registers a new user. Public; requires sign-up enabled in config.
Returns AuthResponse with tokens + (browser callers) Set-Cookie headers.

authorizerv1AdminMeta.description: AdminMeta mirrors model.AdminMeta exactly (no version field).

authorizerv1Meta.description:
Meta mirrors the GraphQL Meta type — server feature flags + provider
availability, returned by the Meta query.

email.description: Either email or phone_number is always present.

app_data.description: Free-form key/value bag — same as GraphQL `app_data: Map`.

authorizerv1User.description:
User mirrors the GraphQL User type. Returned by Profile and embedded in
AuthResponse.

protobufNullValue.description:
`NullValue` is a singleton enumeration to represent the null value for the
`Value` type union.

The JSON representation for `NullValue` is JSON `null`.

 - NULL_VALUE: Null value.

v1AppData.description:
AppData is a free-form key/value bag stored against a user. Mirrors the
GraphQL `Map` scalar. Values are JSON-typed (string, number, bool, null,
nested object, nested array) to match what the existing storage layer
accepts.

authenticator_scanner_image.description:
TOTP enrolment artifacts (populated only when this AuthResponse
initiates TOTP setup; one-time, never re-shown).

v1AuthResponse.description:
AuthResponse mirrors the GraphQL AuthResponse type. Returned (wrapped) by
every method that produces a session: Signup, Login, MagicLinkLogin,
VerifyEmail, VerifyOtp, Session.

checks.description: The checks to evaluate; at least one, at most 100.

user.description:
Optional explicit subject ("type:id", or a bare id treated as
"user:<id>"). Honored only for super-admins or when it equals the
caller's own token subject; anything else is rejected.

results.description: One result per supplied check, in order.

v1FgaRelationInput.description:
FgaRelationInput is a (relation, object) requirement evaluated against the
authenticated caller during Session / ValidateJwtToken / ValidateSession.
AND semantics, fail-closed. Mirrors the GraphQL FgaRelationInput type.

v1FgaTupleInput.description:
FgaTupleInput is one relationship tuple supplied as request-scoped context
for a permission check; contextual tuples are never persisted. Mirrors the
GraphQL FgaTupleInput type.

should_show_mobile_otp_screen.description: For SMS-driven flows the UI may need to render an OTP entry screen.

user.description: Optional explicit subject; same trust rules as CheckPermissionsRequest.

objects.description: Distinct fully-qualified object ids the subject can access.

permissions.description:
The (object, relation) detail — relevant when no relation filter was
supplied.

truncated.description:
True when the result was capped (1000 entries) and more permissions
exist.

next_page_token.description: Opaque cursor for the next page; empty when no more pages.

page.description: 1-based page number. Ignored when page_token is set. Default 1.

limit.description: Page size. Server enforces an upper bound (typically 100). Default 10.

page_token.description:
Opaque cursor returned by the previous List call's `next_page_token`.
Preferred for new clients (AIP-158). When set, `page` is ignored.

v1Permission.description:
Permission is one (object, relation) pair the subject holds: "subject has
`relation` on `object`". Mirrors the GraphQL Permission type.

v1PermissionCheckInput.description:
PermissionCheckInput is one permission to evaluate: "does the subject have
<relation> on <object>?". Mirrors the GraphQL PermissionCheckInput type.

v1PermissionCheckResult.description:
PermissionCheckResult is the outcome of one permission check, echoing the
checked pair so batch results are self-describing (and positionally
aligned). Mirrors the GraphQL PermissionCheckResult type.

token.description: For email flows: the token from the reset email. For SMS flows: the OTP.

required_relations.title:
Optional fine-grained authorization gate: each (relation, object) is
checked against the authenticated caller with AND semantics, fail-closed.
Requires fine-grained authorization enabled

v1UpdateUserRequest.description:
UpdateUserRequest mirrors model.UpdateUserRequest. Nullable GraphQL inputs use
proto3 `optional` so an unset field is distinguishable from an empty value
(e.g. clearing a name vs leaving it untouched, or email_verified=false).

v1UserRequest.description: UserRequest fetches a single user by id or email; at least one must be set.

required_relations.description: Optional fine-grained authorization gate (AND semantics, fail-closed).
Optional fine-grained authorization gate (AND semantics, fail-closed).

email.description: Exactly one of email / phone_number is required.
